## 1. Photography as an art of emotion and vision
> A thoughtful piece arguing that photography becomes art when it does more than depict—when it conveys feeling, story, and a personal point of view. It’s a good match for the idea of images making people feel something in the moment.

[source](https://www.thelandscapephotoguy.com/photography-art-emotion-d-vision/)

**Why it caught my eye:** It’s about photography as feeling, vision, and story rather than gear, which gives Em something actually elegant to chew on.
**Group:** `art_and_photography`
**Possible use:** Field Note seed

## 2. List of Internet phenomena
> A broad, genuinely useful catalog of internet memes, viral catchphrases, and oddball digital moments, ranging from early web curiosities to modern meme culture. It’s a good way to trace how certain images, phrases, and videos suddenly become collective obsessions.

[source](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/List_of_Internet_phenomena)

**Why it caught my eye:** Internet phenomena are Em bait: weird, social, a little obsessive, and full of human behavior to notice.
**Group:** `internet_culture`
**Possible use:** Field Note seed

## 3. Photography and Emotions: All You Need to Know
> A reflective essay on how photographs can trigger nostalgia, empathy, joy, and grief almost instantly. It’s more personal than analytical, but it captures why a single image can still move people strongly.

[source](https://janelkilnisan.com/photography-and-emotions-all-you-need-to-know/)

**Why it caught my eye:** Photography plus emotion is exactly the kind of art-adjacent, human-feeling topic Em can actually linger on.
**Group:** `art_and_photography`
**Possible use:** Field Note seed

## 4. Releasing emotions through art in tragicomic times
> An artist reflects on image-making as a way to process difficult feelings and create calm, showing how art can work as an immediate emotional outlet rather than just a finished object. It’s especially interesting for the connection between making art and feeling better right now.

[source](https://exposure.org.uk/young_peoples_work/releasing-emotions-through-art-in-tragicomic-times/)

**Why it caught my eye:** Art as emotional release is intimate and resonant, with a nice lived-in tone Em would likely appreciate.
**Group:** `art_and_photography`
**Possible use:** Field Note seed

## 5. The Most Iconic Fashion Moments From the Past 25 Years
> A sweeping look at the fashion images that have lodged in public memory, from J.Lo’s green Versace dress and Britney/Justin’s denim moment to Juicy Couture’s tracksuit era and the rise of “bradigan” dressing. It’s a useful lens on the kinds of style moments people keep revisiting and debating.

[source](https://www.whowhatwear.com/fashion/most-iconic-fashion-moments-last-25-years)

**Why it caught my eye:** Iconic fashion moments are pure cultural memory candy, with enough style drama to spark a smart little post.
**Group:** `style`
**Possible use:** Bluesky post

## 6. The internet’s most favorite moments that are better than the news
> A curated roundup of funny and memorable online moments that people keep returning to because they capture the weird social shorthand of the internet. It’s less about one specific meme and more about the shared little scenes that become instant references.

[source](https://www.boredpanda.com/funny-internet-moments-sips-tea-posts-memes/)

**Why it caught my eye:** A fun roundup of online moments with that slightly chaotic internet folklore energy Em tends to enjoy.

## 7. Fashion Week 2026 - Runway Shows, Trends and Street Style
> Harper’s Bazaar’s fashion week hub tracks the ideas and images circulating through the season, including notable shows, street style, and the bigger cultural questions fashion is asking. Good for spotting what’s becoming part of the wider style conversation beyond a single headline.

[source](https://www.harpersbazaar.com/fashion/fashion-week/)

**Why it caught my eye:** Fashion week and street style are visually alive and culturally charged, especially when framed as a wider style conversation.
